solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def minSwaps(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        n = len(nums)
        ones = sum(nums)
        if ones == 0: return 0
        if ones == n: return 0
        left = 0
        right = ones - 1
        swaps = 0
        while left <= right:
            if nums[left] == 1:
                left += 1
            else:
                nums[left], nums[right] = nums[right], nums[left]
                right -= 1
                swaps += 1
        return swaps
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a two-pointer technique to group all 1's together. It maintains two pointers, one at the start and one at the end of the ones group. If the left pointer points to a 1, it moves to the right. If it points to a 0, it swaps the 0 with the 1 at the right pointer and moves the right pointer to the left. This process continues until all 1's are grouped together. The number of swaps is the minimum number of swaps required.
